title: Research Engineer/Research Scientist, Audio
description:
Anthropic seeks researchers to advance audio capabilities within large language models. The Audio team develops systems that understand and generate speech and audio with emphasis on safety, steerability, and robustness.

Key Responsibilities

- Work across the complete audio ML stack, including codec development and data synthesis
- Train large-scale speech language models and audio diffusion models
- Design novel architectures for incorporating continuous signals into LLMs
- Develop conversational systems, speech understanding models, and synthesis capabilities
- Collaborate with teams across pretraining, finetuning, reinforcement learning, and production

Required Qualifications

- Hands-on experience training audio models (speech-to-speech, recognition, synthesis, etc.)
- Balanced interest in both research and engineering (~50/50 split)
- Proficiency across abstraction levels, from signal processing to large-scale training
- Deep expertise with JAX, PyTorch, or distributed training frameworks
- Bachelor's degree in related field or equivalent experience
- Strong communication and cross-team collaboration skills

Preferred Experience

- Large language model pretraining/finetuning
- Diffusion model training
- Reinforcement learning for LLMs
- System optimization and infrastructure (Kubernetes, GPU management)

Anthropic sponsors visas and encourages applications from underrepresented groups.
